quit.substances.tobacco.name: Tobacco / Nicotine
quit.substances.tobacco.trackers.cigaret: Morning cigarettes are the last habit standing. Try a 15-minute walk immediately after waking.
quit.substances.tobacco.trackers.craving: After-meal cravings are nearly gone. Morning accounts for 73% of your remaining pattern.
quit.substances.tobacco.trackers.respira: From winded at 2 minutes to 12 minutes of exercise. Real lung recovery happening.
quit.substances.tobacco.trackers.mood-ir: Irritability down 62% since Day 3. Mood consistently above 7 now.
quit.substances.tobacco.trackers.nrt.ins: Getting 14mg from NRT vs 40mg when fully smoking. 65% nicotine reduction.
quit.substances.tobacco.trackers.financi: Taste and smell returned on Day 7. Lung cancer risk drops 72% at 10 years.
quit.substances.tobacco.calculator.title: Cigarette Cost & Health Impact
quit.substances.tobacco.activities.cravi: Nicotine cravings last 3-5 minutes. Smash this one into dust.
quit.substances.tobacco.activities.lung-: Watch your lungs heal in real time — from the first hour to one year smoke-free.
Hour 1: Carbon monoxide is leaving your bloodstream. Oxygen levels are already rising.
Day 3: Your bronchial tubes are relaxing. Breathing becomes easier. Energy increases.
Week 2: Cilia — the tiny hairs in your lungs — are regrowing. They sweep out mucus and debris.
Month 1: Lung function has increased measurably. Coughing and shortness of breath decrease.
Month 9: Your lungs are dramatically cleaner. Infection risk has dropped. You can feel the difference.
Year 1: Your risk of heart disease is now HALF that of a smoker. Your body chose healing. You let it.

quit.substances.opioids.activities.pain-: Separate real pain from craving. Your body knows the difference — learn to listen.
Is there a headache, or is it mental fog from craving? Notice without judging. Cravings feel like urgency. Pain feels specific.
Opioid withdrawal hits the gut hard — cramping, nausea. Breathe into this area. This sensation is temporary and means your body is recalibrating.
Goosebumps, sweating, sensitivity — your nervous system is resetting. Touch something textured. Ground into sensation.
Anxiety or physical tightness? Place both hands here. Slow your breathing. Your heart rate is normalizing with each sober day.
quit.substances.opioids.activities.safe-: Internalize these truths. They become stronger with repetition.
I am more than my addiction.
My pain is real, but opioids made it worse long-term.
I deserve to feel — even when it\

quit.substances.stimulants.name: Stimulants
quit.substances.stimulants.trackers.use-: 14 consecutive days clean. The physical crash phase is behind you.
quit.substances.stimulants.trackers.mood: Anhedonia resolves over weeks. Dopamine pathways are actively rebuilding.
quit.substances.stimulants.trackers.slee: Sleep pattern normalizing after crash hypersomnia phase.
quit.substances.stimulants.trackers.phys: Heart rate normalized from 118 BPM to 72 BPM.
quit.substances.stimulants.activities.do: Day 1: Your dopamine receptors are nearly empty. Everything feels grey. This is the crash — not your new reality.
Week 1: Your brain begins upregulating receptors. Small pleasures start registering — food, warmth, a funny moment.
Week 2: Anhedonia is fading. Music sounds better. Sunsets have color again. Your reward system is rebuilding.
Month 1: Natural dopamine spikes from exercise, achievement, and connection feel real again.
Month 3: Your baseline has shifted. Normal life has texture and reward. The grey phase is behind you.

quit.substances.benzodiazepines.name: Benzodiazepines
quit.substances.benzodiazepines.trackers: Brain fog decreased 45% since starting the taper.
Symptom burden decreasing with each taper step.
quit.substances.benzodiazepines.activiti: This specific breathing ratio activates your GABA system — the same pathway benzos target.
Inhale through your nose for 4 counts. Fill your belly, not your chest.
Hold gently for 2 counts. No strain.
Exhale through pursed lips for 8 counts. The long exhale is the key — it stimulates your vagus nerve.
Pause for 2 counts. Let your body settle before the next breath.
Map where withdrawal is showing up today. Awareness reduces the fear.
Depersonalization, brain zaps, dizziness? These are GABA receptors recalibrating. It feels terrifying but it\
Visual disturbances, light sensitivity? Your visual cortex is hyperexcitable without benzos dampening it. This normalizes.
Tension, twitching, jaw clenching? Your muscles forgot how to relax naturally. Gentle stretching helps them remember.
Nausea, loss of appetite? Your gut has GABA receptors too. They\
Tingling, burning skin, internal vibrations? Your nervous system is unmuting itself. Each day the volume turns down slightly.
Sudden return of anxiety that was gone yesterday
Enjoying a meal and tasting it fully
Insomnia after a week of good sleep

Schedule a doctor visit, try a stretch routine, call a therapist...
